metadata.name: Fading 3 — Blastoderm ETB: enters with 3 fade counters
metadata.description: Tests the ETB moment of the Fading 3 lifecycle for Blastoderm ({2}{G}{G}, 5/5 Beast with Shroud and Fading 3). p1 casts Blastoderm; the spell resolves with the Fading 3 replacement effect placing 3 fade counters on it as it enters (CR 702.32a ability 1). Blastoderm has no ETB triggered ability, so after a single priority_round (both players pass) the spell resolves and the permanent lands on the battlefield. The script asserts: (a) Blastoderm moves from hand to stack on cast, (b) Blastoderm is on the battlefield after resolution, (c) the stack is empty. The upkeep counter-removal lifecycle (3 upkeeps each removing 1 fade counter, then sacrifice if unable to remove) is better covered by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/fading.rs and is documented in the informational lifecycle section of this script.
metadata.generation_notes: Blastoderm card definition (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/blastoderm.rs): mana_cost {2}{G}{G} (generic:2, green:2), types Creature — Beast, power 5, toughness 5. Abilities: AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Shroud), AbilityDefinition::Fading { count: 3 }. CR 702.32a defines two abilities for Fading N: (1) static replacement — 'This permanent enters with N fade counters on it'; (2) upkeep triggered — 'At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a fade counter from this permanent. If you can't, sacrifice the permanent.' Blastoderm has no ETB triggered ability, so the resolution is straightforward — the permanent enters with 3 fade counters and nothing else fires. Mana: {2}{G}{G} = colorless:2 + green:2. The harness does not support asserting counter counts via 'permanent.Blastoderm.counters.fade' because parse_counter_type() does not map 'fade' to CounterType::Fade (same gap as CounterType::Time for Vanishing/Suspend scripts). The presence of 3 fade counters on ETB is validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/fading.rs. The upkeep lifecycle (remove counter each upkeep; sacrifice on inability to remove) cannot be driven by a single harness script because the harness cannot advance turns to a later Upkeep step. Script scope is intentionally limited to cast + resolve + assert battlefield presence. Analogous to script_stack_149 (Vanishing 3 — Aven Riftwatcher).

disputes.description: Harness gap: parse_counter_type() in replay_harness.rs does not map 'fade' to CounterType::Fade. As a result, assertions of the form 'permanent.Blastoderm.counters.fade: 3' cannot be evaluated after ETB. The same gap applies to CounterType::Time (noted in scripts 136, 149). The fix is to add '"fade" => Some(CounterType::Fade)' to the parse_counter_type() match arm. Full fade counter placement (3 counters on ETB) should be validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/fading.rs (CR 702.32a ability 1).

script.step_note: p1 casts Blastoderm ({2}{G}{G}) from hand. Both players pass priority. The spell resolves: the Fading 3 replacement effect (CR 702.32a ability 1) places 3 fade counters on Blastoderm as it enters the battlefield. Blastoderm has no ETB triggered ability, so the stack is empty and Blastoderm is on the battlefield immediately after resolution.

actions.description: Initial: Blastoderm in p1 hand; p1 at 40 life; battlefield empty; stack empty
actions.note: Blastoderm ({2}{G}{G}, 5/5 Beast, Shroud, Fading 3) is in p1's hand. p1 has {2}{G}{G} (colorless:2, green:2) in mana pool ready to cast.

CR 601.2: p1 casts Blastoderm; {2}{G}{G} paid from pool (colorless:2, green:2). Blastoderm moves from hand to stack. CR 702.32a ability 1: as the permanent enters the battlefield on resolution, the Fading 3 replacement effect will place 3 fade counters on it.

actions.description: After cast: Blastoderm on stack (count 1); p1 hand empty
actions.note: CR 601.2: Blastoderm is now on the stack awaiting resolution.

CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Blastoderm resolves (CR 608.2). CR 702.32a ability 1 (replacement): the permanent enters with 3 fade counters. Blastoderm has no ETB triggered ability, so the stack is empty immediately after resolution.

resolution.note: CR 702.32a ability 1: Fading 3 replacement effect — 'This permanent enters with 3 fade counters on it.' Blastoderm enters with 3 fade counters. NOTE: fade counter assertions blocked by harness gap (see disputes) — should be validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/fading.rs.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Blastoderm spell resolves. The permanent enters with 3 fade counters (Fading 3 replacement, CR 702.32a ability 1). No ETB triggered ability. Stack is empty after resolution.

actions.description: Final: Blastoderm on p1 battlefield (with 3 fade counters, not directly assertable — see disputes); stack empty; both players at 40 life
actions.note: CR 702.32a ability 1: Blastoderm entered with 3 fade counters (to be validated by unit test). Blastoderm is on the battlefield and NOT in the graveyard (sacrifice happens only after 3 upkeeps if unable to remove — see informational lifecycle below). Stack is empty. Neither player's life total changed (Blastoderm has no ETB life effect).

script.step_note: INFORMATIONAL — Fading 3 counter-removal and sacrifice lifecycle documentation. These stack_resolve actions are informational only (no engine commands sent, no assertions checked). The full lifecycle (3 upkeep counter removals + sacrifice) is validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/fading.rs.

resolution.note: CR 702.32a ability 2: 'At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a fade counter from this permanent. If you can't, sacrifice the permanent.' Counter removed: 3 -> 2. Blastoderm remains on battlefield (still has counters). To be validated by unit test in crates/engine/tests/fading.rs.

actions.note: INFORMATIONAL: First upkeep — FadingCounterTrigger resolves (3 -> 2 fade counters). No sacrifice (counters remain).

resolution.note: CR 702.32a ability 2: Second upkeep counter-removal trigger. Counter removed: 2 -> 1. Blastoderm still on battlefield with 1 fade counter.

actions.note: INFORMATIONAL: Second upkeep — FadingCounterTrigger resolves (2 -> 1 fade counter). Blastoderm still alive.

resolution.note: CR 702.32a ability 2: Third upkeep. Last counter removed: 1 -> 0. No more fade counters remain.

actions.note: INFORMATIONAL: Third upkeep — last fade counter removed (1 -> 0).

resolution.note: CR 701.17a: 'To sacrifice a permanent, its controller moves it from the battlefield to its owner's graveyard.' CR 702.32a ability 2: 'If you can't [remove a fade counter], sacrifice the permanent.' At the beginning of the 4th upkeep, no fade counters remain — the trigger fires but cannot remove a counter, so Blastoderm is sacrificed and moves to p1's graveyard. To be validated by unit test in crates/engine/tests/fading.rs.

actions.note: INFORMATIONAL: FadingCounterTrigger fires on 4th upkeep; no counters to remove — Blastoderm sacrificed (CR 701.17a, 702.32a). Full Fading 3 lifecycle complete: ETB (3 fade counters) -> 3 upkeeps remove counters -> sacrifice on 4th upkeep when unable to remove.
